# Decompiled Circuit: TinyLlama Layer 0, KV Head 2, K Projection

**First known full decompilation of an attention head from a production LLM.**

## Summary

TinyLlama 1.1B's layer 0 KV head 2 K projection implements a **multi-script language/content classifier** that routes attention based on token script type (Latin, CJK, Cyrillic, Arabic, code, URL).

The circuit is extremely sparse: each of the 64 output dimensions is a weighted sum of ~3-12 specific embedding dimensions out of 2048 (99.4-99.8% sparse), with the largest weight in the entire model (-3.109) concentrated on a single Unicode/non-Latin feature detector.

## Discovery Method

1. `nd intmap` scanned all 201 GGUF tensors for block entropy
2. `blk.0.attn_k.weight` flagged as 7.3σ outlier (entropy 2.508 vs 4.17 mean)
3. Per-head analysis revealed head 2 has max|w|=3.109 (3x any other head)
4. Sparse decomposition of K_h2 into embedding dim readouts
5. Token-level analysis of what each embedding dimension encodes

## Key Embedding Features Used

| Dim | Positive end | Negative end | Role |
|-----|-------------|-------------|------|
| **411** | Unicode/CJK (жения, ッ, ，) | Code/LaTeX (#!/, forall) | **Primary script detector** |
| **802** | Unicode/Arabic (ι, ّ, ت) | LaTeX/code (documentclass, pragma) | Script vs markup |
| **1454** | CJK/katakana (ッ, デ, ー) | LaTeX citations (citep, cite) | CJK detector |
| **1447** | Code syntax ([`, :`, []{) | CJK punctuation (？, ！, 。) | Syntax classifier |
| **95** | Proper names (Johnny, Bob) | Math terms (cluster, convex) | Content type |
| **687** | Java code (javax, springframework) | CJK/punctuation | Framework code |
| **297** | Code operators (.=, ->, !==) | German/Slavic (Saison, Bahn) | Operator syntax |
| **275** | Structured text (listade, ),() | Cyrillic (ња, ља) | Formatting |

## The Circuit (Decompiled)

Two dominant dimensions with the model's largest weights:

```
k[46] = -3.109*d411 + 1.180*d1454 - 0.848*d95 + 0.750*d802
        - 0.750*d687 + 0.737*d1447 + 0.648*d1482 - 0.645*d297
        - 0.625*d1661 + ...  (23 terms, 70% energy)

k[49] = -2.453*d411 + 0.617*d1454 + 0.617*d1447 + 0.543*d802
        - 0.512*d275 - 0.508*d297 - 0.500*d95 + ...  (12 terms, 67% energy)
```

**Interpretation:** These dimensions produce strongly negative K scores for non-Latin tokens. When the Q projection produces a matching query, attention is routed preferentially to Latin/English tokens — effectively implementing a script-based attention gate.

## Functional Roles of K Output Dimensions

| K dim | Function |
|-------|----------|
| k[46] | Primary script classifier (anti-Unicode gate) |
| k[49] | Secondary script classifier |
| k[28] | Token-type detector (EOS/BOM/code vs Cyrillic) |
| k[42] | Programming language detector (Python/C++/JS) |
| k[37] | Code framework detector (inverse) |
| k[27] | URL/web content detector |
| k[45] | Pro-Unicode gate (opposite of k[46]) |

## Quantitative Structure

- **Sparsity:** 99.4-99.8% (3-23 out of 2048 dims active per K output)
- **Energy capture:** Sparse terms capture 70% of k[46]'s energy, 67% of k[49]
- **Block entropy:** 2.508 bits (vs 4.0 max for Q4_0, 4.17 mean across layers)
- **Head specialization:** Head 2 mean|w| = 0.029 (3x other heads in same layer)

## Method

Generated by `nd intmap` — part of the Neural Decompiler toolchain.
Model: TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0 (Q4_0 quantization).
